text:
there are roughly no moderates left on the democratic side that actually matter.

you’ll have a much easier time trying to understand the current / future political dynamics if you internalize this one basic fact.

RT @signulll: i often have a hard time believing women have to negotiate an outfit every morning. 

i own 12 pairs of the same white shoes,…

there are so many ppl on insta now that just party & travel (& get paid for it somehow).

that’s kinda nuts for everyone else because they have to watch these ppl while they’re at work.

no wonder everyone wants to become an “influencer”.

the internet made ppl infinitely visible, searchable, comparable, & replaceable in just 15 or so years.

therefore ppl are now subject to the same inflationary / deflationary principles as any other good/service. you can see this happening now in terms of how ppl value themselves.

i assume adding personality to the equation would make it more complicated but that’s not immediately parseable from broadcasty online interactions.
the biggest thing to watch now is whether the frontier keeps regenerating scarcity faster than commoditization erodes it.

the most anti signal behavior imaginable is reading the new york times to find out what counts as high signal lmao.

inadvertent irony from the world’s most credentialed slop factory. https://t.co/pYlPBWTHRJ

if you’re genuinely great at what you do & visibly love doing it ppl will be able to tell instantly no matter the medium.

& once they feel that you’re basically going to be impossible to rip off or copy in any meaningful way. you can often copy or mimic skills. taste can kinda be imitated too. formats can def be stolen (ask evan).

but there’s ain’t no xerox machine for pure joy.
why do so many tasteful ppl gravitate towards the color black?

it’s fascinating how much modern internet driven capitalism turns everything into a slop bowl.

starting with chipotle, through to infinite salad places, etc. 

if you think deeply it makes a ton of sense because the bowl is the perfect capitalist vessel.. it’s:

- incredibly modular (swap proteins/bases, same labor flow)
- easily swappable labor
- optimized for throughput (assembly is linear, no plating skills required)
- margin friendly (+ tons of room for upsells)
- instagram native (overhead shot, radial food composition, screams “healthy”)

this same dynamic is now proliferating across froyo which is effectively yet another sorta premium slop bowl (+ insane lines too boot) which also costs upwards of $15-20 per instance.

pretty fascinating to see internet driven convergence of every dynamic.

sorry you will only be able to get into heaven if your follower count is 500k or higher on x, or 1m or higher on instagram.

they are no longer taking any lowbies.

i’m very curious what tbpn metrics are these days.

i never ever see them on the timeline anymore.
what’s the point of going out if you’re going to end up back home anyway?

kinda crazy how society holds technology & ppl to very different standards esp for quality & risk.

e.g. self driving cars have to be near perfect with zero risk when an average human driver is pretty horrendous. ai has to never hallucinate, be incredibly precise, write perfect prose without any patterns, & never make mistakes when the average human has off days, gets sick, & isn’t even that good at these tasks in the first place etc.

in order to make progress, we need to forgive technology in the same way we often forgive humans. like we don’t expect every human to be immaculate although i guess we do when we are looking for love.

great companies & products take time to craft.

early on they are rarely clean, obvious, or even easily tamed. everything feels like a kind of gradient… & at some point after enough iteration + time the picture has the potential to start to come into focus.

you have to do this over & over & over again. with product mechanics, team mechanics, distribution mechanics, taste, judgment, etc until something actually clicks. speed matters a lot. but so does patience. very few ppl can actually live with both of these simultaneously but that’s where the art lies.

& this all applies to not just building/investing but relationships too.

the reality is that most ppl are terrible writers in general.

it’s often way better to read ai writing in this scenario than the slop that an average human produces when they attempt to write anything.
no one is richer than the person who wants little & thanks much.

say what you want about the policy, which is extremely bad, but his presentation & communication skills are genuinely next level. 

the ad is ironically warm (esp with the filter) while communicating a “freeze”, it’s also dead simple, emotionally precise, & incredibly effective. 

terrible economics, excellent execution. credit where credit is due. but this is also pretty dangerous.

lately my favorite recent insta comment that ppl post on each others posts is:

“happiness looks so good on you xo ily”

i don’t know why this is hilarious to me but it’s just a very interesting thing to say. something about the way it is phrased.
RT @signulll: there will never be someone who will bring you more joy in your life than an intelligent witty human who simply does things u…

wishlist for the macbook ultra:

- oled
- touchscreen
- pencil support
- face id
- cellular
- ipad/iphone apps that run with zero friction

this device would collapse the ipad, macbook, workstation, travel machine, & casual media device into a single daily driver.

ideal setup:

- loaded macbook ultra
- iphone 18 pro
- studio display xdr

at that point you basically don’t need any other hardware. apple’s main problem is that the ipad is already the shape of this future, but macos is still the place where a ton of real work lives.
